title: High_Temperature_Fast_Response_Aerodynamic_Probe
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's the breakdown of technical content from the image provided:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Kp = (P2-P3)/(P1-Pm)
*   Ky = (P1-P4)/(P1-Pm)
*   Kptot = (Ptot-P1)/(P1-Pm)
*   Kpsat = (P1-Pstat)/(P1-Pm)
*   Pm = (P2+P3)/2

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 2**
    *   Axes: x, y, z
    *   Variables: pitch, yaw

**3. Tables:**

*   Table 1 not included.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 1:**
    *   FRAP-HT probe

*   **Figure 2:**
    *   4 Sensor Probe: p1, p2, p3, p4
    *   2 Sensor Probe in virtual 4-sensor mode: p1 & p4, p2
    *   Flow Vector: V
    *   Pitch(+), Yaw(-)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Probe width: 1.3 mm
*   Probe thickness: 0.4 mm
*   Linearity up to: 473 K
*   AuAl2 formation: 448 K for 5-6h
*   RTV silicon elastomer withstands up to: 533 K
*   Probe head diameter: 2.5 mm
*   Yaw angle tap distance: 3.5 mm from the probe tip
*   Pitch sensitive tap distance 0.8 mm from the probe tip.
*   Pitch/Yaw sensitive tap diameter: 0.35 mm
*   Current source: 1 mA
*   Amplification factor: 100
*   Temperature window: 303 K to 473 K
*   Pressure Sensitivity: 4.35 mV/(mbar mA)
*   Temperature Sensitivity: 0.38 mV/K
*   Calibration range: Yaw ±24 deg, Pitch ±20 deg
* Calibration Mach number of 0.5.

title: High_Temperature_Fast_Response_Aerodynamic_Probe
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   f^(5/3)PSD(f)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 3 (Left):**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Pressure [mbar]
        *   y: Temperature [K]
    *   Curves: Represents typical output as function of temperature/pressure
    *   Key values: x: 100 to 500 and y 320 to 460
*   **Figure 3 (Right):**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Pressure [mbar]
        *   y: Temperature [K]
        *  Vots [V] 0.4 to 0.55
    *   Curves: Represents typical output as function of temperature/pressure
*   **Figure 4:**
    *   Four graphs (KPHI, KGAMMA, KT, KS)
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Yaw Axis [Deg.]
        *   y: Pitch Axis [Deg.]
    *   Curves: Aerodynamic calibration coefficient curves

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: FRAP-HT calibration model accuracy for Ma=0.5 and calibration range ±24 deg for yaw and ±20 deg for pitch**
    *   Headers: Parameter, FRAP-HT accuracy, % of dynamic head
    *   Data:
        *   φ: ±0.06 deg
        *   ψ: ±0.13 deg
        *   Ptot: ±41 Pa, 0.25
        *   Pstat: ±39 Pa, 0.23
        *   M: ±0.18%

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 5: Piezoresistive miniature silicon chip including electrical schematic of Wheatstone bridge working principle**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Four Resistors: R1, R2, R3, R4
        *   Excitation Current I
        *   Excitation Voltage
        *   Output Signal U

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Natural frequency of sensor membrane: 710 kHz
*   Calibration range: yaw ±24 deg and pitch ±20 deg
*   Accuracy ranges found in Table 1
*   Ma = 0.5

title: High_Temperature_Fast_Response_Aerodynamic_Probe
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
* none visible

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
**Figure 6: Amplitude response of both sensors of the FRAP-HT probe**
*   Axes:
    *   Y-axis:  20*log(f^5/3 PSD / fo^5/3 PSD0) [dB]
    *   X-axis:  Frequency [kHz]
*   Curves:
    *   Yaw Sensor (blue)
    *   Pitch Sensor (pink)
* Key Values:
    * 3dB Difference between curves.
    * Peaks at 36 kHz
    * Flat out to 25 kHz

**3. Tables:**
*none visible

**4. Diagrams:**
*   **Figure 7: FRAP-HT probe shaft**
    *   Components (Top View):
        *   Electronic connection pad
        *   Strain Gauge
        * Probe Shaft (6 mm Diameter)
    *   Components (Bottom View):
        *   Electronic connection pad
        *   PT100
        * Probe Shaft (6 mm Diameter)
*   **Figure 8: Cylindrical four-hole probe.**
        * No labeled components
**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters:**

*   Probe shaft diameter: 6 mm
*   FRAP probe diameter: 1.8 mm
*   Pressure Tap Distance to Probe Tip (Yaw): 2.8 mm at 0 deg
*   Pressure Tap Distance to Probe Tip (Pitch): 0.6 mm at 45 deg
*   Probe shaft slot depth: 0.5 mm
* Pneumatic Four-Hole Probe Calibration Range: ±30 deg yaw, ±24 deg pitch
*   Normalization frequency range: 8-18 kHz
* Cut-off frequency of 25 kHz for measurement bandwidth
* Sensor distance from the tip of the probe: 100mm

    *   **Data:**

        *   varphi / ±1.1 / ±1.0 / ±0.9
        *   gamma / ±2.0 / ±1.7 / ±1.6

*   **Table 3: Main parameters of LISA 1.5 stage axial turbine research facility at design operating point (measured)**
    *   **Headers:** Turbine
    *   **Data:**

        *   Rotor speed (RPM) / 2700
        *   Pressure ratio (1.5 stage, total-to-static) / 1.65
        *   Turbine entry temperature (K) / 314
        *   Total inlet pressure (bar abs norm) / 1.4
        *   Mass flow (kg/s) / 11.8
        *   Hub/tip diameter (mm) / 660/800
*   **Table 4: Characteristic geometry and performance parameters of the 1.5 stage turbine configuration (performance values are derived from pneumatic probe measurements at the design operating point)**
    *   **Headers:** | | Stator 1 | Rotor | Stator 2
    *   **Data:**

        *   No. of blades / 36 / 54 / 36
        *   Inlet flow angle (deg) (midspan) / 0 / 54 / -42

        *   Aspect ratio (span/chord) / 0.87 / 1.17 / 0.82

**4. Diagrams**

*   **Figure 9: Close-up view of the one-and-a-half stage unshrouded axial research turbine equipped with a hot streak generator (inlet duct and vane-shaped support strut)**

        *   Stator 2
        *   Rotor exit plane

        *   Stator 1 exit plane

        *   Hot gas
        *   Support strut
        *   MF
        *   170 mm (length dimension)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Hot streak generator inlet duct diameter: 22 mm
*   Hot streak generator exit wall thickness: 0.5 mm
*   Stator axial chords upstream of the first nozzle guide vane: 3.5
*   Spanwise measurement locations: 5

title: High_Temperature_Fast_Response_Aerodynamic_Probe
text:

Here's the extracted technical content from the gas turbine research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Ttot,mav/TMF

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 10:** Normalized total temperature Ttot,mav/TMF measured with FRAP-HT at the inlet of stator 1 (pitchwise mass-averaged) for configurations 2 and 3.
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Ttot,mav/TMF [-] (from 0.95 to 1.2)
        *   Y-axis: Span [-] (from 0 to 1)
    *   Curves:
        *   50% Span HSG Position
        *   20% Span HSG Position
        *   Key Values: Max temperatures for 50%: 1.1  and for 20%: 1.18

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 5:** Invested test cases for different hot streak temperatures and different spanwise injection positions
    *   Headers:
        *   Conf.
        *   TMF (K)
        *   THS (K)
        *   THS/TMF
        *   HSG span position (%)
    *   Data:
        *   Conf. 1: TMF = 314, THS=378, THS/TMF =1.21, HSG=50%
        *   Conf. 2: TMF = 314, THS=429, THS/TMF =1.36, HSG=50%
        *   Conf. 3: TMF = 314, THS=429, THS/TMF =1.36, HSG=20%

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_DIAGRAMS

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Ttot/TMF=1.36
*   -0.05 stator pitch position
*   Yaw angle difference for FRAP: -0.65 deg to 0.5 deg
*   Yaw angle difference for FRAP-HT: -0.55 deg to 0.4 deg
*   Pitch angle difference for FRAP: -1.2 deg to -0.25 deg
*   Pitch angle difference for FRAP-HT: -0.8 deg to 0.25 deg
*   Level of deviation in Ptot: 0.003
*   Level of deviation in Pstat: 0.007
*   t/T=0.41
*   Stator passage vortex can be found at between 80% and 90% span.

title: High_Temperature_Fast_Response_Aerodynamic_Probe
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content on that gas turbine research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Munk and Prim principle (mentioned in the text)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 12(a):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Ttot/TMF [-]
        *   x-axis: Stator Pitch [-]
    *   Curves:
        *   50% Span HSG Position
        *   20% Span HSG Position
    *   Key values: Ttot/TMF varies from 0.95 to 1.45; Stator Pitch varies from -0.5 to 0.5
*   **Fig. 12(b):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Flow Yaw Angle [°]
        *   x-axis: Stator Pitch [-]
    *   Curves:
        *   50% Span HSG Position
        *   20% Span HSG Position
    *   Key values: Flow Yaw Angle varies from -6 to 6; Stator Pitch varies from -0.5 to 0.5
*   **Fig. 13(a):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Span [-]
        *   x-axis: Angle Deviation [°]
    *   Curves:
        *   Yaw (4HP-FRAP)
        *   Yaw (4HP-FRAP HT)
        *   Pitch (4HP-FRAP)
        *   Pitch (4HP-FRAP HT)
    *   Key values: Span varies from 0 to 1; Angle Deviation varies from -2.5 to 1.5
*   **Fig. 13(b):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Span [-]
        *   x-axis: Pressure Deviation [-]
    *   Curves:
        *   Pstat (4HP-FRAP)
        *   Pstat (4HP-FRAP HT)
        *    Ptot (4HP-FRAP)
        *   Ptot (4HP-FRAP HT)

    *   Key values: Span varies from 0 to 1; Pressure Deviation varies from -0.015 to 0.02

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Blade count ratio: 2:3
*   Temperature range: up to 533 K (500°F)
*   Cut-off frequency: 25 kHz
*   Flow angles: ±24 deg in yaw and ±20 deg in pitch
*   Hot gas area located at around 20% span
*   Axial 1.5 stage turbine facility
